title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Living plan improving package. Update items ship.

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Improve accuracy iteratively, one PR per improvement, merged master. Measure every detection change benchmark (Rscript data-raw/benchmark/run_all.R); merge metrics improve sensitivity regress. Accuracy coverage. Keep name rtransparent now (revisit rename release). self-contained: reimplement oddpub’s data/code detection natively (GPL-3), vendor oddpub (AGPL-3). Fetch article XML NCBI PubMed Central (EFetch + OAI, R/rt_fetch.R), Europe PMC (different corpus, incompatible JATS flavor).

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: #1 v0.3.0 baseline: novelty/replication detectors, registry expansion (ISRCTN/ANZCTR/DRKS/IRCT/UMIN), testthat suite. #2 oddpub/tokenizers made optional (Suggests + guards); NCBI PMC fetch helper R/rt_fetch.R. #3 accuracy benchmark (R/benchmark.R, data-raw/benchmark/, inst/benchmark/); fixed .reroot_xml (returned empty non-OAI XML) unqualified str_detect funding detector. #4 funding specificity 78% → 96% tightening get_fund_acknow_new.

text: quest-bih/rtransparent ahead 7 commits serghiou. - prospero detection fix — ported (PROSPERO id now 5 11 digits, TXT + PMC). benchmark delta (held-set PROSPERO-cases). - coi update (TXT rt_coi, 75 lines) — deferred: TXT path, exercised PMC benchmark; revisit TXT benchmark added. - register pipe update (808 + 686 line reformat register/xml_utils/utils) — skipped: cosmetic, conflicts heavily line’s divergence. - “harmonize new oddpub” — moot reimplement data/code (item B).

what: B. Reimplement oddpub data/code detection natively (GPL-3) [done, tuning recall]
title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Native detector R/data_code.R (.detect_data_code): field-specific accession schemes, repository URLs/names, deposit/availability + DAS language, supplement file-format signals, reuse non-availability vetoes. rt_data_code / rt_data_code_pmc / rt_data_code_pmc_list now use longer need oddpub/tokenizers runtime. Benchmark (data-raw/benchmark/run_data_code.R, inst/benchmark/results_data_code.md): - code 68% sens / 94% spec (beats paper’s ~59% sensitivity). - data 64% sens / 95% spec (precision matches oddpub’s ~97%; recall oddpub’s ~84% long tail supplement-data rare phrasings). Remaining: lift data recall past oddpub (extend supplement/file-format DAS phrasings; ~5 misses extraction gaps, ~21 pattern-tail, ~15 detectable text signal). delete now-dead oddpub/tokenizers helper functions rt_data_code_pmc.R drop Suggests.

title: Detecting disclosure of generative-AI use
text: Since 2023, journals publishers (ICMJE, COPE, Nature, Science, Elsevier others) asked authors disclose use generative AI ChatGPT, GPT-4, Copilot, Gemini large language models preparing manuscript. rt_ai_pmc() detects whether article carries disclosure. deliberately narrow. targets AI used prepare manuscript (writing, editing, language, figures), counts directions disclosure: positive disclosure: “authors used ChatGPT improve readability manuscript”; negative disclosure: “generative AI used preparation work”; dedicated section: “Declaration generative AI” heading. count AI article’s research method (example paper trains deep-learning classifier, one studies ChatGPT subject). mention AI heavily make statement using AI write paper, disclosures. negative lookahead also keeps tool sense “large language model” read something authors edited.

title: Detecting disclosure of generative-AI use
text: practice exist 2023, evaluating older articles meaningless. rt_ai_pmc() therefore reads publication year : returns is_ai_pred = NA articles 2023 (reports year); returns TRUE FALSE 2023 onward. bundled example article 2020, returns NA: three possible values easy act : TRUE (disclosure found), FALSE (article 2023 later carries disclosure), NA (article predates practice assessed). rt_summary() drops NAs, corpus prevalence computed articles indicator applies.
code: xml_path <- system.file(   "extdata", "PMID32171256-PMC7071725.xml", package = "rtransparent" ) ai <- rt_ai_pmc(xml_path, remove_ns = TRUE) c(year = ai$year, is_ai_pred = ai$is_ai_pred) #>       year is_ai_pred  #>       2020         NA

title: Detecting disclosure of generative-AI use
text: indicator new, corpus-level story trend: fast disclosure adopted 2023 onward. bundled simulated corpus rt_demo includes is_ai_pred column (NA 2023) illustration. 2023 onward carries denominator; earlier years assessable articles filtered . Plotted, adoption curve point indicator (rt_plot() drops empty earlier years automatically):  also sits naturally next indicators single prevalence chart; AI bar simply reflects 2023-onward subset:
code: data(rt_demo) ai_by_year <- rt_summary(rt_demo, by = "year", indicators = "is_ai_pred") # Years before 2023 have no assessable articles (all NA), so no denominator; # keep only the years where the indicator applies. ai_by_year <- ai_by_year[ai_by_year$n_articles > 0, ] knitr::kable(   ai_by_year[, c("year", "n_articles", "n_detected", "percent")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Year", "Assessed", "Disclosed", "%") ) library(ggplot2) rt_plot(rt_demo, type = "trend", year = "year", indicators = "is_ai_pred") +   ggtitle("Disclosure of generative-AI use (simulated corpus)") rt_plot(rt_demo) + ggtitle("Transparency indicators, including AI-use disclosure")

title: Detecting disclosure of generative-AI use
text: manual validation 1,000 open-access PMC articles (almost 2024-2026), indicator flagged 16% articles, inspected positives genuine disclosures. main thing intentionally avoids treating articles discuss ChatGPT topic, use AI research method, disclosures: statements manuscript written. every indicator, rt_ai_pmc() returns matched text (ai_text) positive call can always inspected. indicators corpus-summary tools used , see Introduction Summarizing transparency across corpus articles.

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: rtransparent identifies extracts indicators transparency full text published biomedical articles. works two inputs: plain TXT files (typically converted PDFs) PMC XML files (JATS XML served PubMed Central). indicator returns whether indicator found , found, sentence statement triggered detection. rt_all_pmc runs eight detectors together single pass: COI, funding, registration, novelty, replication, data sharing, code sharing AI-use disclosure. (rt_all covers first five TXT; data, code AI PMC XML , last year gate needs publication date.) AI-use disclosure newest indicator. Journals asked authors disclose use generative AI (ChatGPT similar) preparing manuscript since 2023, rt_ai_pmc evaluates indicator articles published 2023 later returns NA earlier ones. package validation described Serghiou et al., Assessment transparency indicators across biomedical literature: open open? (PLOS Biology, 2021, doi:10.1371/journal.pbio.3001107).

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: PMC XML parsed xml2. XML root standardized <article> node (package accepts OAI-PMH, EFetch <pmc-articleset> bare <article> shapes), namespace optionally stripped (remove_ns = TRUE), text split sections indicator usually appears: acknowledgments, footnotes / author notes, body, methods, abstract supplementary material. TXT files read whole split paragraphs.

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: Detection rule-based interpretable: indicator curated set regular expressions applied relevant sections, rather machine learning model. keeps output auditable (matched statement returned) reproducible. Conflicts interest. Detected structured COI footnotes (fn-type = "conflict"), section titles (“Conflicts interest”, “Competing interests”, “Declaration interest”, “Duality interest”), set text patterns covering financial relationships, consulting, fees, board membership, patents explicit “competing interests” declarations. Honoraria--subjects reference text masked reduce false positives. Funding. Detected XML <funding-group> element, funding section titles, text patterns “supported ”, “funded ”, “grant / number”, named funders award types. Acknowledged funding required use explicit funding language (funding verb tied funder), bare mention institution word “support” enough. -funding declarations excluded. Protocol registration. Detected registry identifiers (ClinicalTrials.gov NCT, PROSPERO CRD, ISRCTN, ANZCTR ACTRN, DRKS, IRCT, UMIN, ChiCTR) registration phrasing methods footnotes. Novelty replication. Detected claim patterns “first time”, “knowledge”, “novel finding” (novelty) “replicate”, “independently validated”, “confirmatory cohort” (replication), negation filters (“failed replicate”). Data code sharing. Detected native detector (.detect_data_code) built public repository facts curated benchmark statements: field-specific accession schemes (GEO GSE, SRA / BioProject PRJNA, PDB, ArrayExpress, dbGaP, ProteomeXchange, Dryad / Zenodo / figshare DOIs, …), repository URLs names, deposit / availability / data-availability-statement language, supplement file-format signals. Crucially distinguishes sharing (“data deposited GEO”) reuse (“data downloaded GEO”) excludes “available request”. Code repositories (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket) count data paired data noun, code-GitHub link mistaken data sharing. AI-use disclosure. Detected “Declaration generative AI” type section title, text names generative-AI tool (ChatGPT, GPT-4, Copilot, Gemini, LLM, …) manuscript-preparation context (“used ChatGPT improve readability”) explicit negation (“generative AI used”). negative lookahead keeps tool sense “large language model” writing-object pattern, AI used purely research method (writing) counted. evaluated 2023 onward.

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: rt_summary() reports, indicator, many articles assessed, many positive, apparent prevalence 95% confidence interval:
code: s <- rt_summary(rt_demo) knitr::kable(   s[, c("label", "n_articles", "n_detected", "percent", "conf_low", "conf_high")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Indicator", "Assessed", "Detected", "%", "CI low", "CI high") )

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: text-mining detector perfect, observed prevalence biased estimate true prevalence. rt_summary() corrects using detector’s sensitivity specificity estimates (Rogan-Gladen estimator). correction default adds adj_percent, adj_low adj_high: accuracy values come rt_accuracy: Replication AI-use disclosure bundled accuracy estimates , corrected values NA. Novelty’s estimate comes hand-labeled gold set (inst/benchmark/results_novelty_replication.md); data/code values reproducible benchmark estimates native detector, untouched external-validation estimates. use validation (published oddpub values data code), pass table variable, sensitivity specificity columns:
code: knitr::kable(   s[, c("label", "percent", "adj_percent", "adj_low", "adj_high")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Indicator", "Apparent %", "Corrected %", "CI low", "CI high") ) rt_accuracy #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   variable         label                 sensitivity specificity source          #>   <chr>            <chr>                       <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>           #> 1 is_coi_pred      Conflicts of interest       0.992       0.995 Serghiou et al… #> 2 is_fund_pred     Funding disclosure          0.997       0.981 Serghiou et al… #> 3 is_register_pred Protocol registration       0.955       0.997 Serghiou et al… #> 4 is_open_data     Data sharing                0.765       0.99  rtransparent n… #> 5 is_open_code     Code sharing                0.881       0.995 rtransparent n… my_acc <- rt_accuracy my_acc$sensitivity[my_acc$variable == "is_open_data"] <- 0.758 rt_summary(rt_demo, indicators = "is_open_data", accuracy = my_acc)[,   c("label", "percent", "adj_percent")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   label        percent adj_percent #>   <chr>          <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 Data sharing    20.4        26.0

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: rtransparent R package automatically identifies extracts indicators transparency full text published biomedical articles, TXT (PDF-derived) PMC XML form. indicator returns whether indicator found , found, statement triggered detection. detects seven indicators: Conflicts interest (rt_coi, rt_coi_pmc) Funding (rt_fund, rt_fund_pmc) Protocol registration (rt_register, rt_register_pmc) Novelty claims (rt_novelty, rt_novelty_pmc) Replication components (rt_replication, rt_replication_pmc) Data sharing code sharing (rt_data_code, rt_data_code_pmc) AI disclosure (rt_ai_pmc): use non-use generative AI writing manuscript, evaluated articles published 2023 later rt_all / rt_all_pmc run COI, funding, registration, novelty replication together; rt_meta_pmc returns article metadata PMC XML file. Detection rule-based interpretable (curated regular expressions relevant article sections), output auditable reproducible. See vignette (vignette("rtransparent")) methodology, package website https://choxos.github.io/rtransparent/ full documentation.

title: rtransparent 0.9.0
text: feature release centered novelty replication detectors second, independent validation set. Independent 2023 validation sample. Added held-set 370 open-access PMC articles published 2023, hand-labeled eight transparency indicators (data-raw/benchmark/labels_2023_sample.csv, inst/benchmark/results_2023_sample.md). modern companion Serghiou et al. (2021) held-set, predates indicators 2023-era reporting conventions. conflicts--interest, funding data labels reconciled detector’s extracted statement author’s back matter truncated labeling, three independent detector; novelty, replication, registration code sharing labeled independently meaningful test. Novelty detector improvements. Recall broadened recognize “new” “innovative” (“novel”), much wider set research objects (device, sequence, model, tool, assay, algorithm, variant, isolate, …), passive claims (“novel X developed”), adverbial “first” (“study first provided evidence”), “first ” verbs, “first reported case”. new negation step (.negate_novelty_1) removes firstness attributed cited study (“Smith et al. demonstrated first time”), ordinal/temporal “first” (first-time transplant, first day/week/stage) preserving priority phrase “first time, …”, historical dates (“used first time 1993”). 2023 sample, novelty sensitivity rose 72.8% 77.2% specificity 87.8% 89.6%. Replication detector. Future/conditional replication proposed later work (“study can replicated larger sample”) now treated performed. replication gold set remains small (positives), estimates reported low-power. novelty/replication gold set expanded 160 370 articles, novelty accuracy used rt_summary(accuracy = TRUE) updated accordingly.
